rows.notes: Consensus across all extractors for the header '1873'.
Consensus on header type and no amounts. Visually verified 'Domus' over 'Dorset' (claude-haiku).

Based on gpt-5-mini, which correctly identified 'Brought up' and its associated amount as a distinct total line. Visually confirmed 690 3 0. gemini-flash combined text incorrectly. claude-haiku missed this row entirely.

Visual confirmation of 'Braine' as a header/category line with no associated amount. claude-haiku's classification was most accurate for this specific line. gemini-flash combined it, gpt-5-mini combined it with 'Robey' and missed the category role.

Visually confirmed 'Robey' as an entry description with no explicit monetary amount on its line. claude-haiku incorrectly assigned an amount (6 0 0) to this line, which is not present in the image. gemini-flash combined it, gpt-5-mini combined it with 'Braine'.
Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's accurate reading of 'Hodor' and amount 15 1 0. Other extractors either missed the amount or misread the description and amount.

rows.description: Thomas. & C. Glos & Johnston & C. Legacy 180. Int 11.0.10
rows.notes: Consensus between gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini for the column amount '191 0 10'. The description is taken primarily from gemini-flash for completeness, visually verified. Both correctly identified '180' as part of the description.
Consensus on amount '5 0 4' and identification of embedded arithmetic in description among all three extractors. gemini-flash provided the most accurate description text, visually confirmed.

Consensus on amount '5 0 0' and description among all extractors, with minor textual variations. Used gemini-flash for precise description, visually confirmed.
Visual verification confirms gemini-flash's accurate description 'William Special grant for Do.' and consensus on amount '2 2 0'. gpt-5-mini and claude-haiku read 'Willan'.

Consensus between gemini-flash and gpt-5-mini for the final total amount '10546 0 0.5'. The halfpenny (½) is clearly visible. claude-haiku incorrectly added an extra whole pence ('1') before the fraction.
